evidence.text: Gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles decreased 11% and 12% respectively during the second quarter of 2016 compared to 2015, resulting from an 11% decline in carloadings.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is clearly declining, indicated by significant decreases in ton-miles.

factors.summary: Pricing power is stable or under mild pressure.

evidence.text: Core pricing gains, resource realignments, and productivity gains were more than offset by the impact of lower volume and inflation.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Pricing power is stable but under mild pressure, with mixed factors affecting it.

evidence.text: Gross ton-miles and revenue ton-miles decreased 11% and 12% respectively during the second quarter of 2016 compared to 2015, resulting from an 11% decline in carloadings.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Volumes are clearly declining, as evidenced by significant decreases in ton-miles.

evidence.text: Our second quarter operating ratio increased 1.1 points to 65.2% in 2016 versus 2015 as core pricing gains, resource realignments, and productivity gains were more than offset by the impact of lower volume and inflation.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Margins are compressing, as indicated by an increase in the operating ratio.

evidence.text: Velocity gains resulted from lower volumes, improved network fluidity and a strong resource position. Average terminal dwell time improved 5% and 6% respectively, reflecting the impact of lower volumes and improved network operations.
evidence.subsection: OTHER OPERATING/PERFORMANCE AND FINANCIAL STATISTICS / Operating/Performance Statistics

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving, shown by reductions in terminal dwell time.

evidence.text: The company has a significant share repurchase program and is maintaining capital expenditures, signaling confidence in future growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident, as indicated by share repurchases and capital expenditures.

evidence.text: We expect our capital plan to be approximately $3.675 billion in 2016, which includes Positive Train Control (PTC), but excludes lease buyouts.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Capital expenditures are increasing, signaling investment in growth.

evidence.text: Adjusted debt to capital increased to 47.1% in 2016 from 45.7% in 2015.
evidence.subsection: Debt to Capital / Adjusted Debt to Capital

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Debt leverage is increasing, indicating potential financial strain.

evidence.text: The company has repurchased a total of $17.3 billion of its common stock since the commencement of its repurchase programs in 2007.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shareholder returns are increasing due to significant stock repurchases.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving but decreasing.

evidence.text: Free cash flow for the six months ended June 30, 2016 was $762 million compared to $236 million in 2015.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Free cash flow has improved significantly compared to the previous year.
